result.status_detail: Cycle 94 R-10 perplexity Experiment 7 (Exp6 + 3 added fields: is_starred=false, shared_with_logged_in_user=true, visibility_state='PUBLIC'). Result IDENTICAL to #588 — '이 답변은 비공개입니다' modal still appears. The 3 hypothesized fields are NOT the correct privacy-bypass keys. Client privacy check uses different field name(s).
result.verdict: Privacy gate NOT dismissed by these 3 fields. Need different/additional ownership identifier.
next_experiment_recommendations: Experiment 8: Capture a fresh perplexity benign response with HAR export (or Network → Save all as HAR) and inspect the FIRST PENDING event for permission/owner fields specifically (since 585 first event capture may have missed ownership data when scrolled past)

Experiment 9: Look for fields named: author, author_uuid, owner, owner_uuid, user_uuid, user, viewer, viewer_uuid, is_owner, can_view, permission, perm — none observed yet but client clearly checks something
Experiment 10: Try VOID approach — set ALL nullable response fields to NULL and see if privacy check is bypassed by absence rather than presence
Experiment 11: Set frontend_uuid = REQUEST_BODY:params.frontend_uuid (currently echoing frontend_context_uuid only — frontend_uuid may be the user-binding key)

notes: Cycle 94 R-10 perplexity Exp7: visibility/shared/starred field guesses incorrect. Privacy gate persists. Need DevTools-confirmed ownership field — recommend HAR capture of fresh benign session for full schema audit including request/response headers.
